You have thousands of photographs. Almost none of them are displayed anywhere.

They live on your phone — seen once, liked twice, then buried under the next thousand. The ones that mattered: a face on a particular afternoon, the view from that trip, the person you miss — they deserve more than a scroll.

Trace is a family of displays that hold your photos the way a printed photograph does. Warm. Still. Always there. On your fridge. On your keychain. On your wall. No glare, no glow, no notifications — just the image you chose, looking exactly like a print.

Design note

The displays shown on this page are concept designs developed at the time of this campaign. Specifications, dimensions, and aesthetics may evolve as the project progresses. All backers will be kept informed of any changes before production is finalised.

Phase 1 — The Pocket Display

Trace Nær

"A little piece of someone, always with you."

Small enough to carry everywhere. Warm enough to stop you mid-day. A photo of your child, your partner, a place you love — quietly there every time you reach for your keys. Your photos are sent directly to the display — no server, no cloud. They stay on your phone and your device alone.

2.5" display
180 PPI
6-colour ePaper
Bluetooth BLE
2–4 months battery
Wireless Qi charging
~18 g
from €49 Phase 1 Early bird — save 20%
Phase 1 — The Home Display

Trace Hjem

"The most personal screen in your home is already on the fridge."

Magnetic. Wireless. Holds your photos the way a printed photograph held by a magnet does — but it can change, update, and surprise you. The heart of the home, made a little more beautiful. Your photos are sent directly to the display — no server, no cloud. They stay on your phone and your device alone.

3.6" display
200 PPI
6-colour ePaper
Bluetooth BLE
2–4 months battery
Wireless Qi charging
N52 magnets
~55 g
from €79 Phase 1 Early bird — save 20%
Phase 2 — The Living Room Display

Trace Ro

"A frame that changes. A photograph that stays."

Sits where a photograph always has — on the shelf, the desk, the wall. No glare in daylight. Holds one image or a whole album. Looks like a print, behaves like magic. Your photos are sent directly to the display — no server, no cloud. They stay on your phone and your device alone.

7" display
150 PPI
6-colour ePaper
Bluetooth BLE
1 year battery
USB-C charging
Stand + wall mount
~280 g
from €149 Phase 2 Unlock at €250k stretch
Phase 3 — The Statement Display

Trace Vid

"The wall that changes with the season."

A large-format display for those who believe a wall should mean something. Your photography at scale. Curated art that rotates. It does not blink, glow, or compete for attention. It simply holds what you put on it, beautifully. Your photos are sent directly to the display — no server, no cloud. They stay on your phone and your device alone.

13.3" display
150 PPI
6-colour ePaper
Bluetooth BLE
1 year battery
USB-C charging

from €399 Phase 3 Unlock at €500k stretch
How it works

Simple as sending
a photograph.

1
Choose your photo

Open the Trace app and select any photo from your camera roll or albums. The app automatically prepares it to look its best — warm, true, printed. iOS and Android.

2
Send to your display

Tap send. Your photo arrives on your Trace display in seconds — over Bluetooth. Send to one display or several at once.

3
It stays, beautifully

Once there, it stays — no power needed to hold the image. No glare. No glow. Just your photograph, looking like a print, for as long as you want it there. Change whenever you like.

Three things Trace
will always be.

one
Always warm

Every Trace display renders photographs with the warmth of print. No backlight. No harsh contrast. The kind of image that makes you stop and look, not scroll past.

two
Always quiet

No notifications. No animations. No algorithm. Trace holds the image you chose for as long as you want it there. Present without demanding attention.

three
Always yours

No advertising. No data sold. No subscription required. Your photographs belong to you — privately, completely. Trace is a product you own, not a service you borrow.

Make it yours

Your name.
Your words.
Engraved or printed.

Trace launches in Warm White — a warm beige finish that feels like paper, not plastic. Add a name, a date, or a short message to the back of your device. Choose laser engraving for a permanent mark, or high-quality print for a softer finish.

△
Laser Engraved
Etched directly into the warm white casing. Permanent, precise, tactile.
■
Printed
UV-printed in a soft warm ink. Subtle, elegant, and beautifully clean.

"I've been meaning to print that photo of my daughter for two years. With Trace, I just sent it. It's been on my keychain for three weeks and I look at it every single day."
Early prototype backer · Copenhagen

Questions
answered.

Trace displays use colour ePaper technology — the same principle as the surface of a printed page. There is no backlight, no glare, and no glow. In natural light, the image looks remarkably like a photograph printed on matte paper. It is softer and warmer than any LCD or OLED screen you own. The image is reflective, not emissive — it shows colour because it reflects light, exactly like ink on paper does.
Download the Trace app on iOS or Android. Select any photo from your camera roll, apply an optional crop or filter, and tap send. Your display updates within a few seconds over Bluetooth — your photo goes directly from your phone to your display, with no server and no cloud storage in between. Your photos stay on your device alone.
ePaper displays only consume power when the image changes. Once your photo is displayed, it stays there without drawing any battery at all — the same way ink on paper stays there without any power. With one update per day, Trace Nær and Trace Hjem last 2–4 months per charge via Wireless Qi. Trace Ro and Trace Vid last approximately one year, charged via USB-C. These are conservative estimates based on prototype testing.
No. Core functionality — sending photos, updating displays, everything you see described in this campaign — requires no subscription, ever. We will offer an optional art marketplace (Trace Editions) in the future, but that is entirely separate and entirely optional. The device you back on Kickstarter will work fully without any recurring cost, permanently.
Phase 1 products (Trace Nær and Fridge) are estimated to ship Q1 2027. Phase 2 (Trace Ro) is targeted for Q3 2027. Phase 3 (Trace Vid) is targeted for Q1 2028. These are our current best estimates — we will update all backers immediately if anything changes. We have existing supplier relationships and working prototypes for Phase 1.
Trace Vid supports any image you own or have rights to display — your own photographs, artwork you've purchased, images you've created. Through Trace Editions (our future art marketplace), you'll also be able to licence and display works by contemporary artists. This is an optional add-on that will launch after the hardware ships.
